name: The Jerte Waterfalls - circular from Cabezuela del Valle
name_original: Las Cascadas de Jerte – circular desde Cabezuela del Valle
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Cabezuela Del Valle are set within the Jerte Valley, a region characterized by mountainous terrain, lush forests of oaks and chestnuts, and extensive cherry orchards. The Jerte River flows through the area, contributing to a landscape rich in water features, including numerous natural pools and gorges. The diverse terrain, from river valleys to higher elevations, provides varied conditions for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Cabezuela Del Valle The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Pilones to Jerte - circular through the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve, a difficult 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail that takes about 4 hours to complete. This route explores the unique rock formations and cascades of the Garganta de los Infiernos. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is The Jerte Waterfalls - circular from Cabezuela del Valle, a difficult 21.8 miles (35.1 km) path. This route offers views of the Jerte River's waterfalls and the surrounding valley landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Cabezuela del Valle a El Torno – vuelta por el Río Jerte, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through the Jerte River valley, often completed in about 4 hours 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Cabezuela Del Valle is defined by mountainous terrain, the Jerte River, and the natural features of the Garganta de los Infiernos. The network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cabezuela Del Valle are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cabezuela Del Valle's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cabezuela Del Valle?
A: There are 59 mountain bike trails around Cabezuela Del Valle. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from leisurely rides along the Jerte River to challenging climbs through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Cabezuela Del Valle?
A: The trails cater to various skill levels. You'll find 8 easy routes, 31 moderate routes, and 20 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced mountain bikers can find suitable challenges.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cabezuela Del Valle?
A: Yes, there are 8 easy mountain bike routes around Cabezuela Del Valle that are generally suitable for families. These trails typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation, allowing for a more relaxed experience for all ages.

Q: Can I expect to see waterfalls or natural pools on the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017881090/los-pilones-to-jerte-circular-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones to Jerte - circular through the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, explores the unique rock formations and numerous cascades of the Garganta de los Infiernos. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018263167/the-jerte-waterfalls-circular-from-cabezuela-del-valle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Jerte Waterfalls - circular from Cabezuela del Valle&lt;/a&gt;, offers views of the Jerte River's waterfalls and surrounding valley landscape. The Jerte Valley also boasts several natural swimming areas like El Vao and La Pesquerona.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in the Jerte Valley?
A: Mountain biking in the Jerte Valley offers diverse terrain. You'll encounter mountainous areas, lush forests of oaks and chestnuts, and extensive cherry orchards. Trails often follow the Jerte River, providing scenic riverside paths, but also ascend to higher elevations with more rugged conditions, especially within the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017881090/los-pilones-to-jerte-circular-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones to Jerte - circular through the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018263167/the-jerte-waterfalls-circular-from-cabezuela-del-valle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Jerte Waterfalls - circular from Cabezuela del Valle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking?
A: The trails offer access to several points of interest. You can encounter the unique rock formations of Los Pilones within the Garganta de los Infiernos. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt; waterfall, various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2842817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Honduras Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cabezuela Del Valle?
A: Spring is particularly stunning, as the Jerte Valley transforms with millions of cherry trees in bloom, creating a breathtaking landscape. This season also sees meltwater filling the numerous cascades in the Garganta de los Infiernos. Warmer months are ideal for enjoying the natural pools along the Jerte River. Autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall foliage.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads?
A: While specific dedicated mountain bike parking isn't detailed, Cabezuela del Valle and surrounding villages typically offer public parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage or maps for the most convenient parking spots near your chosen trailhead.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Cabezuela Del Valle?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 80 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Jerte Valley, and the unique features of the Garganta de los Infiernos, including its waterfalls and rock formations.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Cabezuela Del Valle dog-friendly?
A: While the Jerte Valley is a natural area, specific regulations regarding dogs on mountain bike trails are not universally detailed. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural reserves like Garganta de los Infiernos, and to check local park rules or signage at trailheads for any restrictions.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay that are cyclist-friendly in Cabezuela Del Valle?
A: Cabezuela del Valle is a well-established village within the Jerte Valley, offering various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the village. While not explicitly labeled 'cyclist-friendly,' many local establishments are accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts and can cater to their needs.

La Garganta de Marta is another wonderful waterfall in this land of water. There is a car park very close, which means there may be many other people visiting when you visit.

This is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful and impressive waterfalls in the Piornal region. The Caozo waterfall is a wonderful place where you can appreciate nature at its best. It can be reached from Valdastillas following the marked route PR-CC 32.

The interior of Extremadura is full of waterfalls and beautiful waterfalls. This is one of them and it can be easily reached either from Valdastillas via the Caozo waterfall or from Piornal via a picturesque path without difficulty.
